from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase (more concise for common predicates)
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "was started in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorCount": "has",
        "location": "is located in",
        "cost": "costs",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "height": "has a height of",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is located in the",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "bedCount": "has",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "leader is",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "numberOfRooms": "has",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "postal code is",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject to avoid repeating the subject in every clause.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        # Aggregate objects per predicate to allow natural list joining
        pred_objs = {}
        for tr in group:
            pred = tr.predicate
            obj = tr.object.strip('"').strip("'")
            pred_objs.setdefault(pred, []).append(obj)

        parts = []

        # Helper to join list of objects
        def join_objs(objs):
            if not objs:
                return ""
            if len(objs) == 1:
                return objs[0]
            return ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + " and " + objs[-1]

        # Special handling for predicates that need inverted phrasing or custom wording
        if "capital" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"The capital of {subject} is {join_objs(pred_objs['capital'])}")
        if "currency" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"The currency of {subject} is {join_objs(pred_objs['currency'])}")
        if "language" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"The language of {subject} is {join_objs(pred_objs['language'])}")
        if "buildingStartDate" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"Construction of {subject} began in {join_objs(pred_objs['buildingStartDate'])}")
        if "inaugurationDate" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"The inauguration of {subject} was on {join_objs(pred_objs['inaugurationDate'])}")
        if "leader" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"The leader of {subject} is {join_objs(pred_objs['leader'])}")
        if "leaderTitle" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"The leader title of {subject} is {join_objs(pred_objs['leaderTitle'])}")
        if "significantBuilding" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"One of the significant buildings designed by {subject} is {join_objs(pred_objs['significantBuilding'])}")
        if "ethnicGroup" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"One of the ethnic groups of {subject} is {join_objs(pred_objs['ethnicGroup'])}")
        if "origin" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"{join_objs(pred_objs['origin'])} is the hometown of {subject}")
        if "demonym" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"People from {subject} are called {join_objs(pred_objs['demonym'])}")
        if "buildingType" in pred_objs:
            parts.append(f"The {subject} is a {join_objs(pred_objs['buildingType'])} type building")

        # Preferred ordering for remaining common attributes
        preferred_order = ["location", "height", "architect", "completionDate", "floorCount",
                          "floorArea", "cost", "owner", "formerName", "tenant", "isPartOf",
                          "country", "currentTenants", "address", "yearOfConstruction",
                          "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber",
                          "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces",
                          "governingBody", "chancellor", "governmentType", "capital",
                          "leader", "leaderTitle", "currency", "ethnicGroup", "origin",
                          "significantProject", "foundationPlace", "demonym",
                          "numberOfRooms", "keyPerson", "architecture", "postalCode"]
        for pred in preferred_order:
            if pred in pred_objs and pred not in {"capital","currency","language","buildingStartDate",
                                                  "inaugurationDate","leader","leaderTitle",
                                                  "significantBuilding","ethnicGroup","origin",
                                                  "demonym","buildingType"}:
                verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
                objs = join_objs(pred_objs[pred])
                if pred in {"floorCount", "bedCount", "numberOfRooms"}:
                    clause = f"{verb} {objs} floors"
                else:
                    clause = f"{verb} {objs}"
                parts.append(clause)

        # Build sentence for this subject
        if parts:
            # If the first clause already starts with "The", omit repeating the subject
            first = parts[0]
            if first.lower().startswith("the "):
                sentence = ", ".join(parts) + "."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts) + "."
        else:
            sentence = f"{subject}."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine sentences for multiple subjects.
    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    # Capitalize the first character of the whole output.
    if final_text:
        final_text = final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
